barcaui_ai_retention_2025.title: The Impact of Generative {AI} on Learning Retention: A 45-Day Follow-Up Study
barcaui_ai_retention_2025.author: Barcaui, André
barcaui_ai_retention_2025.note: RCT with n=120; 45-day surprise retention test; unscaffolded AI access reduced retention post-removal. Second independent RCT replicating Bastani direction

klein_hollowed_mind_2025.title: The Extended Hollowed Mind: Why Foundational Knowledge Is Indispensable in the Age of {AI}
klein_hollowed_mind_2025.author: Klein, Christian R. and Klein, Reinhard
klein_hollowed_mind_2025.note: PMC12738859. Introduces the ``Sovereignty Trap'' and distinguishes Clark's extended mind from the ``hollowed mind'' produced by uncritical AI dependency. Argues foundational knowledge is prerequisite for legitimate cognitive extension
klein_hollowed_mind_2025.keywords: extended mind, hollowed mind, sovereignty trap, AI dependency

elish_moral_crumple_2019.title: Moral Crumple Zones: Cautionary Tales in Human-Robot Interaction
elish_moral_crumple_2019.author: Elish, Madeleine Clare
elish_moral_crumple_2019.note: Introduces the ``moral crumple zone'': human operators absorbing liability for autonomous systems. Developed from Air France 447 and Uber Tempe fatality case studies
elish_moral_crumple_2019.keywords: moral crumple zone, liability, human-robot interaction

lin_cognitive_integrity_2026.title: Position: Human-Centric {AI} Requires a Minimum Viable Level of Human Understanding
lin_cognitive_integrity_2026.author: Lin, et al.
lin_cognitive_integrity_2026.note: arXiv:2602.00854v1. Submitted to ICML. Defines ``Cognitive Integrity Threshold'' (CIT): minimum Functional Cognitive Integrity to validate AI outputs beyond surface plausibility. Operationalised across healthcare, software engineering, and education
lin_cognitive_integrity_2026.keywords: cognitive integrity threshold, AI safety, human understanding

unesco_ai_education_2021.title: {AI} and Education: Guidance for Policy-Makers
unesco_ai_education_2021.author: {UNESCO}
unesco_ai_education_2021.institution: United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization
unesco_ai_education_2021.note: Explicitly invokes the precautionary principle for AI in classrooms. Notes absence of evidence on how AI affects children's neurological, cognitive, emotional and social development

kestin_ai_tutor_2025.title: {AI} Tutoring Outperforms Active Learning in a Physics Course
kestin_ai_tutor_2025.author: Kestin, Greg and others
kestin_ai_tutor_2025.note: Harvard physics RCT, n=194, effect sizes d=0.73-1.3 in favour of scaffolded AI tutor. No post-removal assessment. Two-week window. Strongest counter-study but tests scaffolded AI, not substitutive

jonas_imperative_responsibility.title: The Imperative of Responsibility: In Search of an Ethics for the Technological Age
jonas_imperative_responsibility.author: Jonas, Hans
jonas_imperative_responsibility.note: Foundational precautionary principle philosophy. When action may produce catastrophic and irreversible consequences, give greater weight to the negative prediction

taleb_precautionary_2014.title: The Precautionary Principle (with Application to the Genetic Engineering of Organisms)
taleb_precautionary_2014.author: Taleb, Nassim Nicholas and Read, Rupert and Douady, Raphael and Norman, Joseph and Bar-Yam, Yaneer
taleb_precautionary_2014.note: arXiv:1410.5787. Formalises precautionary principle for risks that are (a) potentially catastrophic, (b) irreversible, and (c) systemic. Burden of proof falls on those claiming safety when all three conditions hold
taleb_precautionary_2014.keywords: precautionary principle, systemic risk, fat tails

nces_ai_policy_2025.title: Most Public Schools Lack {AI} Policies for Students
nces_ai_policy_2025.author: Kelley, Claire and Holquist, Samantha
nces_ai_policy_2025.note: Child Trends, based on NCES School Pulse Panel data (December 2024). 31\% of public schools had a written AI policy governing student use

casner_retention_2014.title: The Retention of Manual Flying Skills in the Automated Cockpit
casner_retention_2014.author: Casner, Stephen M. and Geven, Richard W. and Recker, Matthias P. and Schooler, Jonathan W.
casner_retention_2014.note: N=16. Motor skills largely retained under autopilot reliance; cognitive executive skills (situation awareness, sequential reasoning) showed more frequent and significant problems. Atrophy is selective and targets higher-order capacities
casner_retention_2014.keywords: automation, skill retention, pilot, cognitive decay

jmir_trust_ai_2025.title: Impact of {AI}-Assisted Diagnosis on American Patients' Trust in and Intention to Seek Help From Health Care Professionals
jmir_trust_ai_2025.author: {JMIR researchers}
jmir_trust_ai_2025.note: PMC12222559. RCT, n=1,762. Explicit no-AI doctor raised intention-to-seek-care to 0.63 vs 0.50 control; AI-assisted dropped to 0.30-0.34. Effect size beta=-0.45, p<0.001, demographically universal

mata_avianca_2023.title: Mata v. Avianca, Inc.
mata_avianca_2023.author: {United States District Court, Southern District of New York}
mata_avianca_2023.note: 678 F. Supp. 3d 443 (S.D.N.Y. 2023). Attorneys fined for submitting ChatGPT-generated briefs with fabricated citations. Established: attorney liability is non-delegable regardless of AI tool use

faa_flightpath_2022.title: Flightpath Management
faa_flightpath_2022.author: {Federal Aviation Administration}
faa_flightpath_2022.note: Advisory Circular AC 120-123. Requires demonstrated manual flight competence as prerequisite before automation reliance is operationally legitimate

aba_opinion_512_2024.title: Formal Opinion 512: Generative Artificial Intelligence Tools
aba_opinion_512_2024.author: {American Bar Association}
aba_opinion_512_2024.note: First formal ABA ethics guidance on AI. Requires ``reasonable understanding of capabilities and limitations'' and capacity for ``independent verification'' before AI reliance. Duty of competence extends fully to AI use

havelock_preface_plato.title: Preface to Plato
havelock_preface_plato.author: Havelock, Eric A.
havelock_preface_plato.note: Argues alphabetic literacy constituted the conditions for abstract, self-reflexive reasoning. The oral-to-literate transition enabled philosophy itself, enhancing domain-general capacities

goody_domestication_1977.title: The Domestication of the Savage Mind
goody_domestication_1977.author: Goody, Jack
goody_domestication_1977.note: Literacy produces new cognitive tools (lists, tables, formulae) enabling systematic critical analysis. Decontextualised knowledge made possible by writing

donald_origins_modern_mind.title: Origins of the Modern Mind: Three Stages in the Evolution of Culture and Cognition
donald_origins_modern_mind.author: Donald, Merlin
donald_origins_modern_mind.note: External Symbolic Storage offloads mimetic-formulaic functions, freeing frontal executive systems for higher-order integration

wolf_reader_come_home.title: Reader, Come Home: The Reading Brain in a Digital World
wolf_reader_come_home.author: Wolf, Maryanne
wolf_reader_come_home.note: Deep reading builds domain-general cognitive capacities (inference, critical analysis, empathy). Digital reading patterns may degrade these circuits. Proposes the ``biliterate brain'' as solution
wolf_reader_come_home.keywords: reading, digital, deep reading, biliterate brain

jandric_postdigital_2018.title: Postdigital Science and Education
jandric_postdigital_2018.author: Jandrić, Petar and Knox, Jeremy and Besley, Tina and Ryberg, Thomas and Suoranta, Juha and Hayes, Sarah
jandric_postdigital_2018.note: Canonical definition: postdigital is ``messy; unpredictable; digital and analog; technological and non-technological; biological and informational''

knox_critiquing_ai_2025.title: Critiquing {AI} in Education
knox_critiquing_ai_2025.author: Knox, Jeremy
knox_critiquing_ai_2025.note: Argues advocates of AI in education engage in substantive speculation through overly optimistic claims; actual risks to learner development are undertheorised

kosmyna_brain_chatgpt_2025.title: Your {{Brain}} on {{ChatGPT}}: {{Accumulation}} of {{Cognitive Debt}} When {{Using}} an {{AI Assistant}} for {{Essay Writing Task}}
kosmyna_brain_chatgpt_2025.author: Kosmyna, Nataliya and Hauptmann, Eugene and Yuan, Ye Tong and Situ, Jessica and Liao, Xian-Hao and Beresnitzky, Ashly Vivian and Braunstein, Iris and Maes, Pattie

bastani_ai_harm_learning_2025.title: Generative {AI} without Guardrails Can Harm Learning: Evidence from High School Mathematics
bastani_ai_harm_learning_2025.author: Bastani, Hamsa and Bastani, Osbert and Sungu, Alp and Ge, Haosen and Kabakci, Ozge and Mariman, Rei
bastani_ai_harm_learning_2025.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
bastani_ai_harm_learning_2025.note: RCT with ~1,000 students: unguarded GPT-4 improved practice by 48\% but harmed post-removal performance; guardrailed GPT Tutor mitigated harm

brookings_ai_students_2026.title: A New Direction for Students in an {AI} World: Prosper, Prepare, Protect
brookings_ai_students_2026.author: Burns, Mary and Winthrop, Rebecca and Luther, Natasha and Venetis, Emma and Karim, Rida
brookings_ai_students_2026.institution: Brookings Institution, Center for Universal Education
brookings_ai_students_2026.note: Global study across 50+ countries concluding AI creates a ``doom loop'' of cognitive dependence; risks currently outweigh benefits
brookings_ai_students_2026.keywords: AI policy, cognitive atrophy, education policy

ma_meta_analysis_ai_2025.title: A Meta-Analysis of the Impact of Generative Artificial Intelligence on Learning Outcomes
ma_meta_analysis_ai_2025.author: Ma, Nan and Zhong, Zhiyong
ma_meta_analysis_ai_2025.note: 34 studies; overall effect size g = 0.68 (p < 0.001); cognitive outcomes strongest (g = 0.795); no long-term or post-removal assessment

lee_ai_critical_thinking_2025.title: The Impact of Generative {AI} on Critical Thinking: Self-Reported Reductions in Cognitive Effort and Confidence Effects
lee_ai_critical_thinking_2025.author: Lee, Hao-Ping and Sarkar, Advait and Tankelevitch, Lev and Drosos, Ian and Rintel, Sean and Banks, Richard and Wilson, Nicholas
lee_ai_critical_thinking_2025.booktitle: Proceedings of the ACM CHI Conference on Human Factors in Computing Systems
lee_ai_critical_thinking_2025.note: Survey of 319 knowledge workers: higher AI confidence correlated with reduced critical thinking engagement
lee_ai_critical_thinking_2025.keywords: critical thinking, automation bias, confidence displacement

jose_cognitive_paradox_2025.title: The Cognitive Paradox of {AI} in Education: Between Enhancement and Erosion
jose_cognitive_paradox_2025.author: Jose, B. and Cherian, J. and Verghis, A. M. and Varghise, S. M. and Joseph, S. and Mumthas, S.
jose_cognitive_paradox_2025.note: AI as both cognitive amplifier and inhibitor; frames the ``cognitive paradox'' of AI improving measurable outcomes while eroding critical thinking capacity
jose_cognitive_paradox_2025.keywords: cognitive paradox, enhancement, erosion, digital cognitive atrophy

clark_extending_minds_ai_2025.title: Extending Minds with Generative {AI}
clark_extending_minds_ai_2025.author: Clark, Andy
clark_extending_minds_ai_2025.note: Clark himself applies extended mind thesis to generative AI; argues LLMs can constitute genuine cognitive extensions; calls for demanding epistemology for hybrid minds
clark_extending_minds_ai_2025.keywords: extended mind, generative AI, cognitive extension

brynjolfsson_ai_work_2025.title: Generative {AI} at Work
brynjolfsson_ai_work_2025.author: Brynjolfsson, Erik and Li, Danielle and Raymond, Lindsey
brynjolfsson_ai_work_2025.note: QJE version: 5,172 customer-support agents; 15\% average productivity gain; least experienced gained ~35\%. NBER working paper (w31161) had 5,179 agents and 14\%; QJE is authoritative

dellacqua_jagged_frontier_2023.title: Navigating the Jagged Technological Frontier: Field Experimental Evidence of the Effects of {AI} on Knowledge Worker Productivity and Quality
dellacqua_jagged_frontier_2023.author: Dell'Acqua, Fabrizio and McFowland III, Edward and Mollick, Ethan R. and Lifshitz-Assaf, Hila and Kellogg, Katherine and Rajendran, Saran and Krayer, Lisa and Candelon, Francois and Lakhani, Karim R.
dellacqua_jagged_frontier_2023.note: Harvard Business School Working Paper 24-013. 758 BCG consultants; 40\% quality gain inside frontier; 19pp worse outside frontier

natali_deskilling_medicine_2025.title: {AI}-Induced Deskilling in Medicine: A Mixed-Method Review and Research Agenda for Healthcare and Beyond
natali_deskilling_medicine_2025.author: Natali, Chiara and Marconi, Luca and Dias Duran, Leslye Denisse and Cabitza, Federico
natali_deskilling_medicine_2025.note: Defines AI-induced deskilling; four vulnerability areas: physical examination, differential diagnosis, clinical judgment, physician-patient communication

rainie_ai_challenge_2026.title: The {AI} Challenge: How College Faculty Assess the Present and Future of Higher Education in the Age of {AI}
rainie_ai_challenge_2026.author: Rainie, Lee and Watson, C. Edward
rainie_ai_challenge_2026.institution: Elon University and American Association of Colleges and Universities
rainie_ai_challenge_2026.note: 1,057 faculty surveyed: 95\% say AI will increase student overreliance; 90\% say it will diminish critical thinking

wang_scaffold_crutch_2024.title: Scaffold or {{Crutch}}? {{Examining College Students}}' {{Use}} and {{Views}} of {{Generative AI Tools}} for {{STEM Education}}
wang_scaffold_crutch_2024.author: Wang, Karen D. and Wu, Zhangyang and Tufts, L'Nard and Wieman, Carl and Salehi, Shima and Haber, Nick

chen_rural_china_ai_2025.title: Advancing Educational Equity in Rural {China}: The Impact of {AI} Devices on Teaching Quality and Learning Outcomes
chen_rural_china_ai_2025.author: Chen and Wu and Chen and Zhou
chen_rural_china_ai_2025.note: Rural students showed 15.69\% improvement vs 10.27\% urban; AI narrows urban-rural gap when deployed with infrastructure

klimova_wellbeing_ai_2025.title: Exploring the Effects of Artificial Intelligence on Student and Academic Well-Being in Higher Education: A Mini-Review
klimova_wellbeing_ai_2025.author: Klimova, Blanka and Pikhart, Marcel
klimova_wellbeing_ai_2025.note: AI benefits (personalized learning, mental health accessibility) alongside concerns: digital fatigue, isolation, anxiety, technostress, diminished interpersonal skills
klimova_wellbeing_ai_2025.keywords: well-being, AI education, technostress, mental health

vanlehn_tutoring_2011.title: The Relative Effectiveness of Human Tutoring, Intelligent Tutoring Systems, and Other Tutoring Systems
vanlehn_tutoring_2011.author: VanLehn, Kurt
vanlehn_tutoring_2011.note: Meta-analysis: ITS effect size d = 0.76 (nearly matching human tutoring d = 0.79)

rand_ai_schools_2025.title: {AI} Use in Schools Is Quickly Increasing but Guidance Lags Behind: Findings from the {RAND} Survey Panels
rand_ai_schools_2025.author: Doss, Christopher Joseph and Bozick, Robert and Schwartz, Heather L. and Chu, Lisa and Rainey, Lydia R. and Woo, Ashley and Reich, Justin and Dukes, Jesse
rand_ai_schools_2025.note: 54\% of K-12 students using AI for school by September 2025 (up 15pp in two years); 45\% of principals reported any school or district AI policy or guidance. The 31\% written-policy figure is from NCES School Pulse Panel (Dec 2024), not this report

letourneau_its_k12_2025.title: A Systematic Review of {AI}-Driven Intelligent Tutoring Systems ({ITS}) in {K}-12 Education
letourneau_its_k12_2025.author: L{\'e}tourneau, Ang{\'e}lique and Martineau, Marion Deslandes and Charland, Patrick and Karran, John Alexander and Boasen, Jared and L{\'e}ger, Pierre Majorique
letourneau_its_k12_2025.note: 28 studies, 4,597 students: ITS effects generally positive; personalization and adaptivity are key drivers; effects attenuated vs non-intelligent technology-based tutoring
letourneau_its_k12_2025.keywords: intelligent tutoring, adaptive learning, K-12, systematic review

brookings_ai_digital_divide_2024.title: {AI} and the Next Digital Divide in Education
brookings_ai_digital_divide_2024.author: Trucano, Michael
brookings_ai_digital_divide_2024.note: Brookings Institution. AI risks creating a third wave of the global digital divide; low- and middle-income countries bear greatest structural disadvantage

burns_ai_tutoring_2026.title: What the Research Shows About Generative {AI} in Tutoring
burns_ai_tutoring_2026.author: Burns, Mary
burns_ai_tutoring_2026.note: Brookings Institution. Synthesizes evidence on generative AI tutoring: where gains are genuine vs hype, and conditions for equitable outcomes

hon_genai_higher_ed_2025.title: Generative {AI} in Higher Education: A Systematic Review of Its Effects on Learning Outcomes and Academic Performance
hon_genai_higher_ed_2025.author: Hon, Kellie (Kai Lai)
hon_genai_higher_ed_2025.note: Systematic review: mixed evidence on GenAI in higher education; some studies report increased engagement, others highlight over-reliance and variable effectiveness across disciplines
hon_genai_higher_ed_2025.keywords: generative AI, higher education, learning outcomes, systematic review

colic_eai_teacher_student_2025.title: The Impact of Generative Artificial Intelligence on Students and Teachers in the Educational Process
colic_eai_teacher_student_2025.author: Colic, Vladan and Sukic, Enes
colic_eai_teacher_student_2025.journal: EAI Endorsed Transactions on Digital Transformation of Industrial Processes
colic_eai_teacher_student_2025.note: GAI offers personalized learning and dynamic content but ~25\% of US teachers report AI does more harm than good in K-12; addresses privacy and ethical concerns
colic_eai_teacher_student_2025.keywords: teacher-student dynamics, generative AI, EAI, digital transformation

bellah_religion_evolution_2011.title: Religion in Human Evolution: From the Paleolithic to the Axial Age
bellah_religion_evolution_2011.author: Bellah, Robert N.
bellah_religion_evolution_2011.publisher: Belknap Press of Harvard University Press
bellah_religion_evolution_2011.note: Draws on Merlin Donald's typology (episodic to mimetic to mythic to theoretic). Axial Age marks breakthrough to abstract, self-reflexive thought. Stages are cumulative, not replacements

instructions_suruppak.title: The Instructions of Suruppak
instructions_suruppak.author: {Anonymous}
instructions_suruppak.note: Sumerian wisdom text, father-to-son admonitions on moral and practical conduct. One of the earliest surviving instruction texts

ptahhotep.title: The Maxims of Ptahhotep
ptahhotep.author: Ptahhotep
ptahhotep.note: Egyptian sebayt (instruction) text. Earliest systematic treatment of communicative competence and ethical conduct

instruction_amenemope.title: The Instruction of Amenemope
instruction_amenemope.author: Amenemope
instruction_amenemope.note: Egyptian wisdom text in thirty chapters. Contrast between the ``silent man'' and the ``passionate man'' as organizing ethical distinction

instruction_ani.title: The Instruction of Ani
instruction_ani.author: Ani
instruction_ani.note: Egyptian instruction text addressed to middle-class scribes, demonstrating democratization of essential knowledge beyond elites

plato_republic.title: The Republic
plato_republic.author: Plato
plato_republic.note: Book VII contains the curriculum for philosopher-guardians: arithmetic, geometry, astronomy, harmonics, dialectic. The ``allegory of the cave'' as a model of cognitive formation

plato_meno.title: Meno
plato_meno.author: Plato
plato_meno.note: Dialogue on the paradox of inquiry and the doctrine of recollection. Genuine knowledge cannot be simply transmitted but must be drawn out through dialectical examination

plato_gorgias.title: Gorgias
plato_gorgias.author: Plato
plato_gorgias.note: Dialogue on rhetoric as mere flattery (kolakeia) versus genuine knowledge. Political stakes of knowledge without philosophical foundation

plato_phaedrus.title: Phaedrus
plato_phaedrus.author: Plato
plato_phaedrus.note: Contains the critique of writing as potentially destructive to memory (274b--278b) and the argument that rhetoric without philosophical knowledge is dangerous

aristotle_nicomachean_ethics.title: Nicomachean Ethics
aristotle_nicomachean_ethics.author: Aristotle
aristotle_nicomachean_ethics.note: Book VI contains the taxonomy of episteme, techne, phronesis, sophia, and nous. Phronesis (practical wisdom) is the master practical virtue, irreducible to rules

aristotle_metaphysics.title: Metaphysics
aristotle_metaphysics.author: Aristotle
aristotle_metaphysics.note: Foundational classification of forms of knowledge. Distinction between experience (empeiria) and wisdom (sophia)

aristotle_organon.title: Organon
aristotle_organon.author: Aristotle
aristotle_organon.note: Logical treatises including the Prior Analytics, establishing formal logic as foundational to all inquiry

confucius_analects.title: The Analects
confucius_analects.author: Confucius
confucius_analects.note: Core Confucian text. Opens with ``Is it not a pleasure to learn and then to practice what you have learned at the right times?'' (1.1). Learning as practice-bound formation, not information acquisition

mencius.title: Mencius
mencius.author: Mencius
mencius.note: Argues human nature is fundamentally good with four innate ``beginnings'' (si duan, 2A.6). The ox-mountain metaphor (6A.8) on depletion of natural goodness

xunzi.title: Xunzi
xunzi.author: Xunzi
xunzi.note: Chapter 19 (Li Lun) on ritual and education as the foundation of formation. Chapter 23 argues human nature is bad and goodness is acquired through rigorous formation

kautilya_arthasastra.title: Arthasastra
kautilya_arthasastra.author: Kautilya
kautilya_arthasastra.note: Governance-oriented curriculum naming the ruler's essential sciences: inquiry/reasoning (anvikshiki), Vedic knowledge, economics, and political science. Practical statecraft as essential knowledge

cicero_de_oratore.title: De Oratore
cicero_de_oratore.author: Marcus Tullius Cicero
cicero_de_oratore.note: The ideal orator must possess genuine knowledge of law, philosophy, history, and human nature. Oratory without philosophical foundation produces only a ``faber linguae'' (artisan of the tongue)

quintilian_institutio.title: Institutio Oratoria
quintilian_institutio.author: Marcus Fabius Quintilianus
quintilian_institutio.note: Most systematic Roman educational treatise. Defines the ideal educated person as vir bonus dicendi peritus (the good man skilled in speaking, 12.1.1). Developmental theory of education

seneca_letters.title: Epistulae Morales ad Lucilium
seneca_letters.author: Lucius Annaeus Seneca
seneca_letters.note: Letter 88 (``On Liberal Studies'') critiques education that produces cultivated superficiality without moral transformation. The only essential knowledge is the art of living (ars vivendi)

marcus_aurelius_meditations.title: Meditations
marcus_aurelius_meditations.author: Marcus Aurelius
marcus_aurelius_meditations.note: Exemplifies the Roman ideal of philosophical cultivation as prerequisite for practical governance. Emperor maintaining rigorous philosophical practice

isocrates_antidosis.title: Antidosis
isocrates_antidosis.author: Isocrates
isocrates_antidosis.note: Argues practical judgment (phronesis) is cultivated through engagement with civic questions and great examples, not abstract philosophy. Rhetoric as civic formation

martianus_capella.title: De Nuptiis Philologiae et Mercurii (The Marriage of Philology and Mercury)
martianus_capella.author: Martianus Capella
martianus_capella.note: Late antique synthesis formalizing the seven liberal arts: trivium (grammar, rhetoric, dialectic) and quadrivium (arithmetic, geometry, music, astronomy)

hugh_didascalicon.title: Didascalicon: De Studio Legendi
hugh_didascalicon.author: Hugh of St Victor
hugh_didascalicon.note: Frames education as pursuit of wisdom and human perfection. Knowledge is a whole; fragmented knowledge is deformed knowledge

alghazali_book_knowledge.title: Ihya Ulum al-Din: Kitab al-Ilm (The Book of Knowledge)
alghazali_book_knowledge.author: Abu Hamid al-Ghazali
alghazali_book_knowledge.note: Classifies knowledge into fard ayn (individually obligatory) and fard kifaya (communally obligatory). Every individual bears personal responsibility for minimum religious and moral knowledge

augustine_confessions.title: Confessions
augustine_confessions.author: Augustine of Hippo
augustine_confessions.note: Model of self-examination and self-knowledge as epistemological and ethical imperative

castiglione_courtier.title: Il Libro del Cortegiano (The Book of the Courtier)
castiglione_courtier.author: Baldassare Castiglione
castiglione_courtier.note: Defines sprezzatura as composed mastery and the ideal of the uomo universale (universal man). Self-regulation and breadth of knowledge for civic life

montaigne_essays.title: Essais
montaigne_essays.author: Michel de Montaigne
montaigne_essays.note: Invention of the self-examining mind. Essayistic self-investigation as a model of metacognition and epistemic humility

bacon_novum_organum.title: Novum Organum
bacon_novum_organum.author: Francis Bacon
bacon_novum_organum.note: ``New instrument'' to replace Aristotle's Organon. Theory of the four ``Idols of the Mind'' (Tribe, Cave, Marketplace, Theatre) as systematic cognitive biases requiring metacognitive correction

bacon_advancement.title: The Advancement of Learning
bacon_advancement.author: Francis Bacon
bacon_advancement.note: Systematic classification of human knowledge into Memory (history), Reason (philosophy), and Imagination (poetry). Foundation for the Encyclopedie's knowledge tree

descartes_discourse.title: Discourse on the Method
descartes_discourse.author: Ren{\'e} Descartes
descartes_discourse.note: Method of systematic doubt. ``Good sense is the most evenly distributed thing in the world.'' The capacity for autonomous epistemic agency as the defining human attribute

locke_essay.title: An Essay Concerning Human Understanding
locke_essay.author: John Locke
locke_essay.note: Tabula rasa: all knowledge derives from experience. Foundation for the Enlightenment theory that human development depends on education, not innate endowment

locke_education.title: Some Thoughts Concerning Education
locke_education.author: John Locke
locke_education.note: Most important educational aim is the formation of the habit of reasoning and rational self-governance, not transmission of particular knowledge

comenius_great_didactic.title: Didactica Magna (The Great Didactic)
comenius_great_didactic.author: Jan Amos Comenius
comenius_great_didactic.note: ``The Whole Art of Teaching All Things to All Men.'' Pansophism: universal education as a human right, not a class privilege. First developmental pedagogy

comenius_orbis_pictus.title: Orbis Sensualium Pictus (The Visible World in Pictures)
comenius_orbis_pictus.author: Jan Amos Comenius
comenius_orbis_pictus.note: First illustrated textbook. Learning connected to sensory experience

rousseau_emile.title: {\'E}mile, ou De l'{\'e}ducation
rousseau_emile.author: Jean-Jacques Rousseau
rousseau_emile.note: Most influential Enlightenment educational treatise. Negative education: removing obstacles to natural development rather than imposing knowledge. Developmental sequence from sensory education to moral formation

kant_education.title: {\"U}ber P{\"a}dagogik (On Education)
kant_education.author: Immanuel Kant
kant_education.note: Four stages: discipline, culture, civilization, moralization. Education must cultivate autonomy (moral self-determination), not compliant output-production

kant_enlightenment.title: Beantwortung Der Frage: {{Was}} Ist Aufklärung? ({{Answering}} the Question: {{What}} Is Enlightenment?)
kant_enlightenment.author: Kant, Immanuel

kant_critique.title: Critique of Pure Reason
kant_critique.author: Immanuel Kant
kant_critique.note: Critical philosophy: before knowing the world reliably, one must understand the structures through which the knowing mind operates. Metacognition as philosophical foundation

diderot_encyclopedie.title: Encyclop{\'e}die, ou Dictionnaire Raisonn{\'e} des Sciences, des Arts et des M{\'e}tiers
diderot_encyclopedie.author: Denis Diderot and Jean le Rond d'Alembert
diderot_encyclopedie.note: 17 volumes of text, 11 of plates. Most ambitious effort to systematize and democratize knowledge. Treats all educated persons as capable of understanding the entire range of human knowledge

jefferson_bill.title: A Bill for the More General Diffusion of Knowledge
jefferson_bill.author: Thomas Jefferson
jefferson_bill.note: Founding document of American public education. ``If a nation expects to be ignorant and free, in a state of civilization, it expects what never was and never will be.'' Illuminating minds prevents tyranny

franklin_proposals.title: Proposals Relating to the Education of Youth in Pensilvania
franklin_proposals.author: Benjamin Franklin
franklin_proposals.note: Frames education as foundational to private and public happiness and civic capacity

condorcet_instruction.title: Rapport et Projet de D{\'e}cret sur l'Organisation G{\'e}n{\'e}rale de l'Instruction Publique
condorcet_instruction.author: Nicolas de Condorcet
condorcet_instruction.note: First systematic plan for state education producing democratic citizens. Intellectual dependence ``inevitably corrupts both parties to the relationship''

smith_wealth_nations.title: An Inquiry into the Nature and Causes of the Wealth of Nations
smith_wealth_nations.author: Adam Smith
smith_wealth_nations.note: Analysis of ``mental mutilation'' from the division of labour. Workers confined to simple operations ``naturally lose the habit of exertion'' and become ``as stupid and ignorant as it is possible for a human creature to become''
smith_wealth_nations.keywords: enlightenment, division of labour, cognitive atrophy

ferguson_civil_society.title: An Essay on the History of Civil Society
ferguson_civil_society.author: Adam Ferguson
ferguson_civil_society.note: Commercial society's specialization and atomization threaten civic bonds. Moral sense and social sympathy as essential knowledge

wang_yangming.title: Instructions for Practical Living and Other Neo-Confucian Writings
wang_yangming.author: Wang Yangming
wang_yangming.note: Doctrine of the unity of knowledge and action (zhi xing he yi). Knowledge is not possessing correct statements but inseparable from embodied moral action and self-cultivation

florentine_codex.title: Historia General de las Cosas de Nueva Espa{\~n}a (Florentine Codex)
florentine_codex.author: Bernardino de Sahag{\'u}n
florentine_codex.note: Central source for Nahua knowledge, rhetoric, and moral philosophy. Books on rhetoric/moral philosophy and elite customs/education demonstrate ethical speech and formation as core cultural knowledge

marx_capital.title: Capital: A Critique of Political Economy
marx_capital.author: Karl Marx
marx_capital.note: Chapter 14: Division of labour in manufacture. Workers become ``annexed'' for life to a partial function; skill narrows and human beings become parts in a productive mechanism

newman_idea_university.title: The Idea of a University
newman_idea_university.author: John Henry Newman
newman_idea_university.note: ``Knowledge its own end.'' Rejects reducing education to immediate utility. Formation of a ``liberal habit of mind'' that sees relations among domains and evaluates claims

dewey_democracy_education.title: Democracy and Education
dewey_democracy_education.author: John Dewey
dewey_democracy_education.note: Education as enabling continued growth. Learning as a living capacity rather than a stockpile of information. The aim of education is the capacity to continue educating oneself

arendt_origins_totalitarianism.title: The Origins of Totalitarianism
arendt_origins_totalitarianism.author: Hannah Arendt
arendt_origins_totalitarianism.note: Analysis of conditions enabling totalitarian rule, including destruction of autonomous judgment and the capacity for independent thought

arendt_eichmann.title: Eichmann in Jerusalem: A Report on the Banality of Evil
arendt_eichmann.author: Hannah Arendt
arendt_eichmann.note: The ``banality of evil'': competent execution of harmful instructions without moral check. Incompetence in moral reasoning as an ethical and political catastrophe
arendt_eichmann.keywords: moral formation, judgment, banality of evil

jaeger_paideia.title: Paideia: The Ideals of Greek Culture
jaeger_paideia.author: Werner Jaeger
jaeger_paideia.note: Three-volume comprehensive analysis of Greek paideia as the process by which a person is formed into the kind of person their culture considers excellent

ryle_concept_mind.title: The Concept of Mind
ryle_concept_mind.author: Gilbert Ryle
ryle_concept_mind.note: Foundational distinction between ``knowing that'' (propositional knowledge) and ``knowing how'' (procedural knowledge)

polanyi_tacit_dimension.title: The Tacit Dimension
polanyi_tacit_dimension.author: Michael Polanyi
polanyi_tacit_dimension.note: ``We can know more than we can tell.'' Tacit knowledge resides in practice and cannot be fully articulated into propositions. Definitionally inaccessible to AI

snow_two_cultures.title: The Two Cultures and the Scientific Revolution
snow_two_cultures.author: C. P. Snow
snow_two_cultures.note: Deep split between scientific and humanistic literacy as a societal handicap for solving public problems

drucker_age_discontinuity.title: The Age of Discontinuity
drucker_age_discontinuity.author: Drucker, Peter F.
drucker_age_discontinuity.note: Expands and popularises the ``knowledge worker'' concept first coined in Landmarks of Tomorrow (1959), not this volume. Economic participation increasingly depends on mental skills: problem-solving, producing and using knowledge, decision-making, autonomy

postman_technopoly.title: Technopoly: The Surrender of Culture to Technology
postman_technopoly.author: Neil Postman
postman_technopoly.note: Societies increasingly take their orders from technology and treat technique as superior to complex human judgment. Media literacy as essential defense

carr_shallows.title: The Shallows: What the Internet Is Doing to Our Brains
carr_shallows.author: Nicholas Carr
carr_shallows.note: Information technologies shape assumptions about intelligence. The Internet encourages rapid, distracted sampling rather than the focused attention associated with print culture

nussbaum_not_for_profit.title: Not for Profit: Why Democracy Needs the Humanities
nussbaum_not_for_profit.author: Martha C. Nussbaum
nussbaum_not_for_profit.note: Democratic citizenship depends on cultivated imagination and the ability to see others as full persons. A ``silent crisis'' where education is reduced to profit-making at the expense of democratic capacities

crawford_shop_class.title: Shop Class as Soulcraft: An Inquiry into the Value of Work
crawford_shop_class.author: Matthew B. Crawford
crawford_shop_class.note: Defense of manual competence and embodied knowledge. The knowledge in a craftsperson's hands as a form of intelligence that resists abstraction

sennett_craftsman.title: The Craftsman
sennett_craftsman.author: Richard Sennett
sennett_craftsman.note: Craftsmanship as a basic human impulse and the desire to do a job well for its own sake. Embodied skill as ethical formation

klein_sources_power.title: Sources of Power: How People Make Decisions
klein_sources_power.author: Gary Klein
klein_sources_power.note: Recognition-Primed Decision Making (RPD). Expert judgment in time-critical situations operates through rapid pattern recognition developed from embodied experience, not deliberate analysis

mcmurtry_cancer_stage.title: The Cancer Stage of Capitalism: From Crisis to Cure
mcmurtry_cancer_stage.author: John McMurtry
mcmurtry_cancer_stage.note: Life-Value Onto-Axiology (LVOA). Grounds moral evaluation in enabling conditions for Thought, Feeling, and Action (T-F-A) as the capacities constituting full human life

mcmurtry_value_wars.title: Value Wars: The Global Market Versus the Life Economy
mcmurtry_value_wars.author: John McMurtry
mcmurtry_value_wars.note: Framework for distinguishing life-value from money-value. The life-ground as the basis for evaluating institutions and technologies

bjork_bjork_2011.title: Making Things Hard on Yourself, but in a Good Way: Creating Desirable Difficulties to Enhance Learning
bjork_bjork_2011.author: Bjork, Elizabeth L. and Bjork, Robert A.
bjork_bjork_2011.booktitle: Psychology and the Real World: Essays Illustrating Fundamental Contributions to Society
bjork_bjork_2011.editor: Gernsbacher, M. A. and Pew, R. W. and Hough, L. M. and Pomerantz, J. R.
bjork_bjork_2011.note: Core desirable difficulties framework. Conditions slowing apparent learning improve long-term retention and transfer because effortful retrieval builds durable storage strength

bjork_bjork_2014.title: Making Things Hard on Yourself, but in a Good Way
bjork_bjork_2014.author: Bjork, Elizabeth L. and Bjork, Robert A.

roediger_karpicke_2006.title: Test-Enhanced Learning: Taking Memory Tests Improves Long-Term Retention
roediger_karpicke_2006.author: Roediger, Henry L. and Karpicke, Jeffrey D.
roediger_karpicke_2006.keywords: testing effect, retrieval practice, learning science

parasuraman_manzey_2010.title: Complacency and Bias in Human Use of Automation: An Attentional Integration
parasuraman_manzey_2010.author: Parasuraman, Raja and Manzey, Dietrich H.
parasuraman_manzey_2010.note: Distinguishes automation bias (over-reliance on automated outputs without independent verification) from automation complacency (reduced monitoring of automated systems over time)

parasuraman_sheridan_wickens_2000.title: A Model for Types and Levels of Human Interaction with Automation
parasuraman_sheridan_wickens_2000.author: Parasuraman, Raja and Sheridan, Thomas B. and Wickens, Christopher D.
parasuraman_sheridan_wickens_2000.journal: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics---Part A

skitka_mosier_burdick_1999.title: Does Automation Bias Decision-Making?
skitka_mosier_burdick_1999.author: Skitka, Linda J. and Mosier, Kathleen and Burdick, Mark D.

kasneci_etal_2023.title: ChatGPT for Good? On Opportunities and Challenges of Large Language Models for Education
kasneci_etal_2023.author: Kasneci, Engin and Se{\ss}ler, Kathrin and K{\"u}chemann, Stefan and Bannert, Maria and Dementieva, Daryna and Fischer, Frank and Gasser, Urs and Groh, Georg and G{\"u}nnemann, Stephan and H{\"u}llermeier, Eyke and Kruber, Stephan and Kuber, Gitta and Lahner, Bastian and Litman, Diane and Marchetti, Sara and Melloni, Irena and Metternich, Michael and Nerdel, Claudia and Peiss, Shirin and Strasser, Thomas
kasneci_etal_2023.note: Systematic review of ChatGPT's opportunities and challenges for education

malinka_etal_2023.title: On the Educational Impact of ChatGPT: Is Artificial Intelligence Ready to Obtain a University Degree?
malinka_etal_2023.author: Malinka, Kamil and Peresini, Martin and Firc, Anton and Huj{\v{n}}{\'a}k, Ond{\v{r}}ej and Jan{\v{c}}a, F.

topol_2019.title: High-Performance Medicine: The Convergence of Human and Artificial Intelligence
topol_2019.author: Topol, Eric J.

cabitza_rasoini_gensini_2017.title: Unintended Consequences of Machine Learning in Medicine
cabitza_rasoini_gensini_2017.author: Cabitza, Federico and Rasoini, Raffaele and Gensini, Gian Franco

oecd_deseco_2005.title: The Definition and Selection of Key Competencies: Executive Summary
oecd_deseco_2005.author: {OECD}
oecd_deseco_2005.institution: Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development
oecd_deseco_2005.note: DeSeCo project defining key competencies for a successful life and well-functioning society: using tools interactively, interacting in heterogeneous groups, acting autonomously

oecd_education_2030.title: OECD Future of Education and Skills 2030
oecd_education_2030.author: {OECD}
oecd_education_2030.institution: Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development
oecd_education_2030.note: Frames student agency, purpose, and navigating unfamiliar contexts as major goals. Metacognition and responsible autonomy at the centre of essential capacities

p21_framework_2009.title: P21 Framework for 21st Century Learning
p21_framework_2009.author: {Partnership for 21st Century Learning}
p21_framework_2009.note: Foregrounds critical thinking, problem solving, communication, and collaboration integrated into core subjects

elman_eight_legged.title: A Cultural History of Civil Examinations in Late Imperial China
elman_eight_legged.author: Benjamin A. Elman
elman_eight_legged.note: Analysis of the eight-legged essay and the imperial civil service examination system. Canonical mastery as gateway to governance

humboldt_linguistic_diversity.title: On Language: On the Diversity of Human Language Construction and its Influence on the Mental Development of the Human Species
humboldt_linguistic_diversity.author: Wilhelm von Humboldt
humboldt_linguistic_diversity.note: Each language represents a distinct worldview. Language learning as cognitive and cultural enlargement irreducible to acquiring a code for the same ideas

ong_orality_literacy.title: Orality and Literacy: The Technologizing of the Word
ong_orality_literacy.author: Walter J. Ong
ong_orality_literacy.note: Writing ``restructured consciousness.'' Analysis of the transition from oral to literate culture and its cognitive implications

clausewitz_on_war.title: Vom Kriege (On War)
clausewitz_on_war.author: Carl von Clausewitz
clausewitz_on_war.note: Coup d'oeil: the ability to see the whole at a glance in the fog of war. Strategic intelligence as a form of practical judgment (phronesis) developed through experience

ruskin_stones_venice.title: The Stones of Venice
ruskin_stones_venice.author: John Ruskin
ruskin_stones_venice.note: ``The Nature of Gothic'' chapter: the human importance of creative freedom and meaningful workmanship denied by industrial standardization

morris_useful_work.title: Useful Work Versus Useless Toil
morris_useful_work.author: William Morris
morris_useful_work.note: Argues for meaningful work as essential to human wellbeing. Industrial labour destroys the craft knowledge and creative engagement that make work humanly worthwhile

vygotsky_mind_society.title: Mind in Society: The Development of Higher Psychological Processes
vygotsky_mind_society.author: Lev S. Vygotsky
vygotsky_mind_society.note: Zone of proximal development (ZPD). Productive struggle at the edge of capacity with appropriate support as the locus of genuine cognitive development
vygotsky_mind_society.keywords: zone of proximal development, developmental psychology

csikszentmihalyi_flow.title: Flow: The Psychology of Optimal Experience
csikszentmihalyi_flow.author: Mihaly Csikszentmihalyi
csikszentmihalyi_flow.note: Engagement with challenging cognitive tasks matched to developing capability as the condition for intrinsically meaningful experience

locke_second_treatise.title: Two Treatises of Government
locke_second_treatise.author: John Locke
locke_second_treatise.note: Legitimate political authority rests on rational consent of governed persons who understand their own rights. The free citizen is necessarily a reasoning person

karpathy_vibe_coding_2025.title: Vibe Coding
karpathy_vibe_coding_2025.author: Andrej Karpathy
karpathy_vibe_coding_2025.note: Coinage and description of ``vibe coding'': AI-assisted development where users accept generated code without reviewing or understanding it. Output without formation

iep_aztec_philosophy.title: Aztec Philosophy
iep_aztec_philosophy.author: {Internet Encyclopedia of Philosophy}
iep_aztec_philosophy.note: Nahua philosophy articulates the ideal of forming ``a wise face and a good heart'' (in ixtli in yollotl). Education as character-making and judgment-shaping

ndpr_huehuetlatolli.title: Review of: Discourses of the Elders---The Aztec Huehuetlatolli: A First English Translation
ndpr_huehuetlatolli.author: {Notre Dame Philosophical Reviews}
ndpr_huehuetlatolli.note: Huehuetlatolli: ``words of the elders,'' elder-to-youth moral guidance placing ethical judgment and rhetorically competent speech at centre of essential skills

britannica_maat.title: Ma'at: Egyptian Goddess
britannica_maat.author: {Encyclopaedia Britannica}
britannica_maat.note: Ma'at: organising concept connecting truth, justice, and cosmic/social order. The standard by which knowledge and conduct are evaluated in Egyptian tradition

britannica_studia_humanitatis.title: Studia Humanitatis
britannica_studia_humanitatis.author: {Encyclopaedia Britannica}
britannica_studia_humanitatis.note: Renaissance humanist curriculum: grammar, rhetoric, poetry, history, moral philosophy, classical languages. Formative of the person rather than merely useful

britannica_madrasah.title: Madrasah
britannica_madrasah.author: {Encyclopaedia Britannica}
britannica_madrasah.note: Islamic educational institution centring Quran, Hadith, Arabic grammar/literature, logic, mathematics. Memorisation and certification (ijazat) as key transmission mechanisms

britannica_griot.title: Griot
britannica_griot.author: {Encyclopaedia Britannica}
britannica_griot.note: West African griot tradition. Oral eloquence as technology of communal memory and identity. The keeper of knowledge bears moral responsibility for its use and transmission

britannica_ottoman_modernisation.title: The Struggle to Modernize the Empire
britannica_ottoman_modernisation.author: {Encyclopaedia Britannica}
britannica_ottoman_modernisation.note: Ottoman reform-era educational modernisation connected to military/technical weakness and need for science/technology alongside religious schooling

mext_terakoya.title: Terakoya Education in the Edo Period
mext_terakoya.author: {Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology (MEXT), Japan}
mext_terakoya.note: Tokugawa-era terakoya: commoner schooling in reading, writing, abacus calculation. Foundational literacy/numeracy plus disciplined practice for everyday and commercial life

robson_tablet_house_2001.title: The Tablet House: A Scribal School in Old Babylonian Nippur
robson_tablet_house_2001.author: Robson, Eleanor
robson_tablet_house_2001.note: Definitive study of Old Babylonian Eduba. Four-phase elementary curriculum documented from 1,425 excavated tablets: writing techniques, thematic noun/lexical lists organised by semantic category, advanced sign and metrological lists, introductory Sumerian

lee_six_arts_2022.title: Sojourning in the Arts: Considering the Implications of the {Confucian} ``Six Arts'' in a Contemporary Educational Context
lee_six_arts_2022.author: Lee, Yen-Yi
lee_six_arts_2022.booktitle: Nature, Art, and Education in {East Asia}: Philosophical Connections
lee_six_arts_2022.note: Overview of Six Arts (rites, music, archery, charioteering, calligraphy, mathematics) as curriculum cultivating character and capability. Treats the Six Arts as ``the Chinese version of liberal education''

springer_civil_examinations.title: Civil Service Examinations in China
springer_civil_examinations.author: {Springer Encyclopedia of Educational Philosophy and Theory}
springer_civil_examinations.note: Imperial examination system making canonical mastery the gateway to governance. Moral-textual literacy embedded as state capacity

kamakoti_fourteen_vidyas.title: The Fourteen Branches of Knowledge (Vidyas)
kamakoti_fourteen_vidyas.author: {Kamakoti.org}
kamakoti_fourteen_vidyas.note: Vedic classification: Fourteen Vidyas integrating Vedas, Vedangas, and other bodies of knowledge into a structured conception of essential knowledge

ganeri_philosophy_classical_india.title: Philosophy in Classical India: The Proper Work of Reason
ganeri_philosophy_classical_india.author: Ganeri, Jonardon
ganeri_philosophy_classical_india.note: Establishes Anviksiki as the systematic discipline of critical inquiry and rational investigation in ancient Indian traditions

oxford_medieval_curriculum.title: III.1: University Curriculum
oxford_medieval_curriculum.author: {Bodleian Library, University of Oxford}
oxford_medieval_curriculum.note: Medieval university curriculum documentation. Trivium and quadrivium as the foundation for all higher learning

parry_singer_tales.title: The Singer of Tales
parry_singer_tales.author: Albert B. Lord
parry_singer_tales.note: Parry-Lord oral-formulaic theory. Oral composition generates different cognitive capacities from literate composition. Loss of generative oral memory as enabling but real cognitive transformation

clark_supersizing_mind.title: Supersizing the Mind: Embodiment, Action, and Cognitive Extension
clark_supersizing_mind.author: Andy Clark
clark_supersizing_mind.note: Extended mind thesis: cognitive processes extend beyond the skull into environmental and technological resources. Philosophical challenge to the ``internalized knowledge'' requirement
clark_supersizing_mind.keywords: extended mind, cognitive extension, philosophy of mind

clark_chalmers_1998.title: The Extended Mind
clark_chalmers_1998.author: Clark, Andy and Chalmers, David J.
clark_chalmers_1998.note: Original extended mind hypothesis. Argues cognitive processes can be partially constituted by external resources under certain conditions

sweller_cognitive_load.title: Cognitive Load Theory
sweller_cognitive_load.author: John Sweller
sweller_cognitive_load.note: Cognitive load theory argues that reducing extraneous cognitive load improves learning. Counter-evidence to the ``all difficulty is desirable'' reading of Bjork

kahneman_thinking.title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
kahneman_thinking.author: Daniel Kahneman
kahneman_thinking.note: System 1 (fast, intuitive) and System 2 (slow, deliberate) thinking. Cognitive biases and heuristics. Relevant to automation bias and critical evaluation

springer_liberal_arts.title: The Liberal Arts Tradition
springer_liberal_arts.author: {Springer Encyclopedia of the History of Education}
springer_liberal_arts.note: Historical analysis of the liberal arts tradition from antiquity through the medieval period

springer_printing_press.title: The Printing Press and the Dissemination of Knowledge
springer_printing_press.author: {Springer Encyclopedia}
springer_printing_press.note: Printing press rapidly increases books produced, lowers access cost, expands literacy. The ecology of knowledge transformed by mechanical reproduction

springer_knowledge_worker.title: The Knowledge Worker in the 21st Century
springer_knowledge_worker.author: {Springer}
springer_knowledge_worker.note: Drucker's knowledge worker concept and its evolution. Economic participation depending on mental skills rather than physical labour

cambridge_morris_ruskin.title: Morris and John Ruskin
cambridge_morris_ruskin.author: {Cambridge Companion to William Morris}
cambridge_morris_ruskin.note: Creative freedom and meaningful workmanship denied by the factory system. Craft critics' argument for the human importance of embodied, skilled work

chiculture_five_thousand_years.title: China: Five Thousand Years of History and Civilization
chiculture_five_thousand_years.author: {Chinese University of Hong Kong}
chiculture_five_thousand_years.note: Context for Ming/Qing examination system and the role of canonical mastery in governance

britannica_renaissance_thought.title: Renaissance Thought
britannica_renaissance_thought.author: {Encyclopaedia Britannica}
britannica_renaissance_thought.note: Humanists emphasized philological criticism and attention to textual accuracy: skills for evaluating sources under expanding information availability

grafton_jardine_humanism.title: From Humanism to the Humanities: Education and the Liberal Arts in Fifteenth- and Sixteenth-Century Europe
grafton_jardine_humanism.author: Grafton, Anthony and Jardine, Lisa
grafton_jardine_humanism.note: Definitive treatment of how Renaissance humanists developed philological criticism for evaluating source authenticity

smarason_beyond_fragmentation.title: Beyond Fragmentation: A Life-Value Alternative for AI Governance
smarason_beyond_fragmentation.author: Sm{\'a}rason, Magn{\'u}s Sm{\'a}ri
smarason_beyond_fragmentation.note: VALOR framework (Verification, Alignment, Legitimacy, Oversight, Responsibility). Introduces Cognitive Debt, Cognitive Threshold, and maps AI governance against McMurtry's Life-Value Onto-Axiology and T-F-A framework
smarason_beyond_fragmentation.keywords: AI governance, VALOR, cognitive debt, LVOA

dreyfus_mind_machine_1986.title: Mind over Machine: The Power of Human Intuition and Expertise in the Era of the Computer
dreyfus_mind_machine_1986.author: Dreyfus, Hubert L. and Dreyfus, Stuart E.
dreyfus_mind_machine_1986.note: Five-stage skill acquisition model (novice, advanced beginner, competent, proficient, expert). Expert performance depends on situational discrimination built through accumulated experience; cannot be accelerated by better information. Connects to Japanese shu-ha-ri pedagogical progression

dreyfus_five_stage_2004.title: The Five-Stage Model of Adult Skill Acquisition
dreyfus_five_stage_2004.author: Dreyfus, Stuart E.
dreyfus_five_stage_2004.journal: Bulletin of Science, Technology and Society
dreyfus_five_stage_2004.note: Most-cited journal version of the Dreyfus skill acquisition model. Progressive shift from rule-dependent analytical decision-making (novice) to intuitive, holistic, context-sensitive performance (expert). Expertise requires embodied experience, not encoded rules

yanagi_unknown_craftsman_1972.title: The Unknown Craftsman: A Japanese Insight into Beauty
yanagi_unknown_craftsman_1972.author: Yanagi, S{\=o}etsu
yanagi_unknown_craftsman_1972.note: Adapted by Bernard Leach. Foundational text of the Japanese mingei (folk craft) movement. Elevates ordinary handmade objects as embodiments of aesthetic and moral quality that machine production cannot replicate. Resistance to industrialisation through craft as a form of human expression

autor_why_jobs_2015.title: Why Are There Still So Many Jobs? The History and Future of Workplace Automation
autor_why_jobs_2015.author: Autor, David H.
autor_why_jobs_2015.note: Task-based framework for technological change. Technology does not eliminate occupations wholesale but restructures them, automating routine tasks and increasing premium on non-routine judgment, adaptability, and cross-domain integration
autor_why_jobs_2015.keywords: automation, labour market, task-based, job polarisation

bainbridge_ironies_1983.title: Ironies of Automation
bainbridge_ironies_1983.author: Bainbridge, Lisanne
bainbridge_ironies_1983.note: Classic paper: the more reliable the automation, the less the human practises the skill, the less competent the human becomes at the moments when automation fails and human judgment is most needed

riaa_vinyl_2024.title: 2024 Year-End Music Industry Revenue Report
riaa_vinyl_2024.author: {Recording Industry Association of America}
riaa_vinyl_2024.note: US vinyl record revenues reached approximately \$1.4 billion in 2024, surpassing CD revenues. Demonstrates counter-market pattern: digital commodification of music created premium market for physical, intentional engagement
